verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically cytoplasmic but robustly translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under stress (heat shock) and in tumor cells via direct phosphatidylserine binding. This stress-induced and cancer-cell-specific surface display is well-documented by cell surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and confocal microscopy. Membrane-bound HSPA1A exerts distinct immunological and pro-survival functions. The protein reaches the outer face by its own lipid-binding mechanism, not passive recruitment.
